formatted_message:
[!] ORCL: Recovery Setup Detected

Down 43.5% from 52-week high.
Quality: [OK] Revenue positive, Debt low, Margin positive.
Momentum: returning. Volume: elevated.
Signal strength: 0.60 / 1.00

Why now?
This pattern has appeared 2,382 times in 7 years of data.
Average 12-month return from similar entries: +49.2%
vs random entry: +22.3% (edge: +26.9 percentage points)

What to expect:
The stock may drop another 10-15% before recovering.
That is normal. Median drawdown before recovery: -15%.
Planned hold: 12 months (~252 trading days).
No stop-loss. The edge requires holding through the dip.

Similar historical entries:
  AVGO Mar 2020: down 48%, signal fired -> +89.9% in 3 months
  TSLA May 2023: down 49%, signal fired -> +61.5% in 3 months
  CRM Dec 2022: down 50%, signal fired -> +53.0% in 3 months

[!] This is not a recommendation. You decide.
This is a statistical pattern, not a guarantee.
1 in 4 similar entries ends negative at 12 months.

Past performance in backtesting does not guarantee future results. This is not financial advice.
